codeReview.summary: The feature includes useful parity fixes and artifacts, but it misses core acceptance requirements for the new visual parity harness: the spec runs against demo simulator routes instead of the live runtime path required by the feature contract, and the baseline source/scale do not match the required Paper MCP scale=2 capture procedure.

issues.description: The parity spec navigates `/demo/${scenarioId}?chrome=0` (simulator fixtures) rather than exercising `/create/profile`, `/create/distribute`, and `/create/complete` on the live relay-pump runtime path. This conflicts with the feature expectedBehavior requiring the spec to validate these surfaces against live runtime (NOT simulator).

The committed baseline metadata explicitly sets `baselineScale` to `1x` and documents that baselines were copied from `public/paper-reference` instead of captured via Paper MCP `get_screenshot(..., scale=2)` as required by the feature procedure. This leaves the implementation out of contract for baseline acquisition.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill/mission guidance is ambiguous for parity reviews: `ui-screen-worker` requires agent-browser visual verification for changed UI, while also allowing limited exceptions; this run skipped interactive browser verification and only used `playwright --list` plus unit tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:23,51,53; mission AGENTS.md:89; handoff file shows `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` and `npx playwright test ... --list` only (handoff lines 31, 83, 87).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission feature metadata contains a transposed Paper artboard mapping for create-profile vs distribute-shares (8GU-0/60R-0), which forced manual reconciliation during execution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mission features.json:4200 maps /create/profile to 8GU-0 and /create/distribute to 60R-0, while commit artifacts and baselines.json document corrected mapping.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit bdadd75. Found two blocking contract mismatches (simulator-based parity spec and non-scale=2 baseline capture path), so this feature review is marked FAIL despite the targeted UI fixes and documentation updates.
